mnemonics.title: Queue Operations
mnemonics.content: Enqueue means ENTER the queue at the back (Rear). Dequeue means DELETE from the front.

mnemonics.title: FIFO Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: First In = First Out. Just like a movie theater line - whoever gets in first gets served first.

mnemonics.title: Circular Queue Full Condition
mnemonics.content: FULL = (Rear + 1) % N == Front. Think: Rear catches up to Front from behind, leaving exactly one empty slot.

acronyms.usage: Principle followed by queue data structure - the first element inserted is the first to be removed
Same as FIFO conceptually, but opposite of Stack's LIFO
Graph traversal algorithm that uses a queue to explore vertices level by level
Position from where elements are removed (dequeued)
Queue that allows insertion/deletion from both ends

visualTips.visualization: Think of a circular queue as a merry-go-round. When you reach the end, you wrap around to the beginning. Draw a circle with front and rear pointers moving clockwise.
Imagine two arrows: Front arrow points to who gets served next, Rear arrow points to where new customer will stand. Both arrows only move forward (to the right). In circular, they can loop back.
Draw a box representing queue. For circular queue, when distinguishing empty vs full, always keep ONE slot empty - this is the visual key!
Draw: [New] → [Rear] ... [Front] → [Out]. New people join at Rear, people leave from Front.

analogies.analogy: A queue is like a line at a ticket counter - people join at the end (rear) and the person at the front gets served first.
Think of a round dining table. When you reach the last seat, you can go back to the first seat if it's empty.
In an emergency room, patients are treated by severity of illness, not by when they arrived - this is a priority queue.
A deque is like a sliding door train where you can enter or exit from either end of the carriage.
Queue is like a FIFO queue (checkout line), Stack is like a LIFO stack (stack of plates in a cafeteria).
